claim: The text-only section's elevated entropy (3.9016 bits, highest of all sections) reflects unenciphered or lightly enciphered natural-language prose, and its word bigram entropy is significantly higher than that of the zodiac and astronomical sections, consistent with natural syntactic variation rather than label repetition.
evidence_for: Text-only entropy 3.9016 bits approaches Latin (~4.0) and Italian (~3.95) glyph entropy, consistent with minimal or no transposition
High-probability belief [0.677] already supports text-only section as closest to natural language
7 folios of continuous prose with no illustrations would naturally have richer syntactic context than diagram labels
evidence_against: Higher entropy could reflect a more complex cipher layer rather than less encoding
Small section size (7 folios, 2,349 words) limits statistical power for bigram entropy estimation
If the text-only section uses a different cipher key, elevated entropy could be artifactual
test: Compute word-level bigram entropy H(w2|w1) for text-only, zodiac, astronomical, and herbal sections; compare section-level bigram entropies using bootstrap confidence intervals; additionally compute the ratio of word bigram entropy to unigram entropy as a syntactic regularity index.
test_metric: Word bigram entropy per section and bigram/unigram entropy ratio
test_threshold: Confirm if text-only bigram entropy exceeds zodiac bigram entropy by â‰¥0.3 bits and the bigram/unigram ratio for text-only is closest to values observed in naturally occurring medieval Latin or Italian; deny if text-only bigram entropy is not the highest among all sections.

tests_run.test: compute word-level bigram entropy h(w2|w1) for text-only, zodiac, astronomical, and herbal sections;
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.
